C[MrCyclops: Game isn't over yet! If you play J5 and then K6 white lives. Black is ahead, sure, but it's not over yet
C[sojiro: here is the invasion at the second liner.I don't know which is better for black. B5/C6.what do you think?is my answer correct?
mrnoob: This is a fairly common invasion on the 4-4 when there is already a keima
column2: usually black will answer either c5 or c3
column3: there is also some other variations like c4 and b6.

column1: sojiro: hmm.. so,C6 is not the best .I should have to push white here,right?but, I play keima instead of push white.
mrnoob: Yes, pushing white out will allow you to attack my group more efficiently.

C[sojiro: I thought I can kill it if you tenuki here and I take A4,right?it's so pity that this group alive.so,it's the way to invade in second line?thanks for the moves.I'll try it in the next game later in the future with another opponents surely.
mrnoob: Yeah, a4 is necessary to live in the corner, but this gives black a lot of influence on the outside.
C[mrnoob: In this case, you should descend at j18 to take away the living space.
sojiro: owh..I never think about J18..Thanks, I got new knowledge again.
C[sojiro: it got directly.I thought that I can kill it,but it already alive right now,right?
mrnoob: This move was to make sure that my group was alive
column2: I am fairly sure it is fine now.

C[sojiro: It really need necessary,right? or not for now?what do you think?
mrnoob: I would say this move is important to make sure that your shape has no weaknesses.
C[mrnoob: I have a weakness in this shape at n2.

mrnoob: Right now would have been good timing for it.
